drugs.notes: Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Vesicant. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 900mg/m². Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils <1x10^9/L or platelets <100x10^9/L on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days until criteria met. May resume at original dose or reduce to 80% depending on clinical circumstances. If second episode or delay >7 days, consider changing treatment or growth factors.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological: neutrophils <1x10^9/L or platelets <100x10^9/L on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days until criteria met. May resume at original dose or reduce to 80% depending on clinical circumstances.

Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L or AST >4x ULN or bilirubin >51 µmol/L

dose_modifications.notes: Dose reduction may not be necessary

Ondansetron 8mg oral or intravenous 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy

special_warnings: Maximum lifetime cumulative dose of epirubicin is 900mg/m²
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting treatment. Baseline LVEF should be measured, particularly in patients with history of cardiac problems or in the elderly.
Discontinue epirubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Epirubicin is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required.
Where intention is to follow with another regimen such as paclitaxel, only FOUR cycles may be necessary - always check on prescribing cycle 1.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval.

Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Inflammitant. Dose banded per national dose bands (25mg/ml). Folinic acid rescue required 24 hours after administration.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days. May resume at original dose or reduce to 80%. Day 8 treatment seldom delayed for low blood counts.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or equivalent IV dose 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on days 1 and 8
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on days 1 and 8

special_warnings: Patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before cycle 1 initiation; complete or partial DPD deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with fluorouracil.
Presence of a third fluid compartment (e.g. ascites, renal failure) may delay methotrexate clearance and increase toxicity.
Folinic acid rescue is required after each dose of methotrexate.
Day 8 treatment is seldom delayed for low blood counts.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.

Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on days 1 and 8

Dexamethasone 4mg oral once a day for 3 days (take home)

special_warnings: Patients must be tested for DPD deficiency before cycle 1 initiation; complete or partial DPD deficiency increases risk of severe and fatal toxicity with fluorouracil.
Presence of a third fluid compartment (e.g. ascites, renal failure) may delay methotrexate clearance and increase toxicity.
Folinic acid rescue is required after each dose of methotrexate.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed when prescribing, dispensing or administering oral chemotherapy.
It must be made clear to all staff, including those in the community, that this is a short course of oral chemotherapy (days 1-14) that must not be continued beyond day 14.
Day 8 treatment is seldom delayed for low blood counts.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Doses >200mg diluted in 500ml NaCl 0.9% (max concentration 0.74mg/ml). Exfoliant. In patients with poor bone marrow reserves, consider starting at 75mg/m² with a view to escalate to 100mg/m² if well tolerated.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia grade 3-4 (previous dose 100mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 then reduce to 75mg/m²

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 then reduce to 60mg/m²

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce from 100mg/m² to 75mg/m² once neuropathy resolves to grade ≤2

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolves to grade ≤1, then reduce: 100→75mg/m² or 75→60mg/m². Stop if at 60mg/m² or no recovery after 2 weeks. Stop for grade 4.

Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >ULN and/or AST/ALT ≥3.5xULN AND Alk Phos ≥6xULN

dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment needed for renal impairment

dose_modifications.condition: Excessive lacrimation/tearing persisting after symptomatic treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 80% of original dose. Symptomatic treatment with hypromellose 0.3% eye drops four times a day first.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel (days -1, 1, 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Growth factor per local formulary: filgrastim 300mcg SC daily days 3-9, OR lenograstim 263mcg SC daily days 3-9, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2
If dexamethasone pre-medication forgotten: dexamethasone 20mg IV bolus once only
special_warnings: Docetaxel is highly myelosuppressive; in patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting at 75mg/m² with view to increase to 100mg/m² if well tolerated.

Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion. Do not interrupt for minor symptoms (flushing, localised rashes). Immediately discontinue for profound hypotension, bronchospasm or generalised erythema.
Dexamethasone pre-medication must be taken for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel to prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity.
Docetaxel-induced fluid retention can cause weight gain - this is not a reason to alter doses.
Excessive lacrimation related to cumulative docetaxel doses (after median 400mg/m²).
Doses >200mg must be diluted in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel (days -1, 1, 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
If dexamethasone pre-medication forgotten: dexamethasone 20mg IV bolus once only
special_warnings: Indication: treatment of locally advanced or metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer that has failed to adequately respond to an anthracycline-containing therapy or when further anthracycline therapy is contra-indicated.
Docetaxel is highly myelosuppressive; in patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting at 55mg/m² with view to increase to 75mg/m² if well tolerated.
Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion. Do not interrupt for minor symptoms. Immediately discontinue for profound hypotension, bronchospasm or generalised erythema.
Dexamethasone pre-medication must be taken for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel to prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity.
Docetaxel-induced fluid retention can cause weight gain - this is not a reason to alter doses.
Excessive lacrimation related to cumulative docetaxel doses (after median 400mg/m²).
Doses >200mg must be diluted in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.

drugs.notes: LOADING DOSE - Cycle 1 only. Day 1 of cycle 1. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Observe for 60 minutes after first infusion. If gap ≥6 weeks between infusions, re-administer 840mg loading dose.

MAINTENANCE DOSE - Cycles 2 onwards. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Observe 30-60 minutes after subsequent infusions. Dose reductions NOT permitted - if intolerable stop drug. No dose adjustment for renal impairment (mild-moderate); safety not established in hepatic impairment.

LOADING DOSE - Cycle 1 only. Day 1 of cycle 1. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 90 minutes. Observe 6 hours after first infusion. If dose missed by >14 days, re-administer 8mg/kg loading over 90 minutes.

MAINTENANCE DOSE - Cycles 2 onwards. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Observe 2 hours after subsequent infusions. Dose reductions NOT permitted - if intolerable stop drug. No dose adjustment for renal impairment. If dose missed ≤14 days, give usual 6mg/kg as soon as possible.

Cycle 1: given on DAY 2. Cycles 2-6: given on Day 1. May be increased to 100mg/m² from cycle 2 onwards if well tolerated. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Doses >200mg in 500ml NaCl 0.9%. Exfoliant.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia grade 3-4 (previous dose 75mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 then reduce to 60mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia (previous dose 60mg/m²)

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until platelets ≥100x10^9/L then reduce to 60mg/m²
No dose modifications for pertuzumab for haematological toxicity. If not tolerated, stop drug.
No dose modifications for trastuzumab for haematological toxicity. If not tolerated, stop drug.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: AST/ALT ≥1.5xULN AND Alk Phos ≥2.5xULN
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >ULN and/or AST/ALT ≥3.5xULN AND Alk Phos ≥6xULN

dose_modifications.notes: Safety and efficacy of pertuzumab not established in hepatic impairment - clinical decision required
Reduce from 75mg/m² to 60mg/m² once neuropathy resolves to grade ≤2. Consider stopping if grade 3 at <75mg/m² or grade 4.

dose_modifications.condition: LVEF <50% or asymptomatic LVEF decline: LVEF <40% or LVEF 40-45% with ≥10% fall from baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Hold pertuzumab and trastuzumab. Repeat LVEF in 3 weeks. Stop if LVEF remains <40% or 40-45% with ≥10% fall. Resume if LVEF ≥45% or 40-45% with <10% fall. Symptomatic cardiac dysfunction: discontinue, commence ACE inhibitor, refer to cardiologist.

dose_modifications.condition: LVEF <50% or asymptomatic LVEF decline: LVEF <40% or LVEF 40-45% with ≥10% fall from baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Hold trastuzumab. Repeat LVEF in 3 weeks. Stop if LVEF remains <40% or 40-45% with ≥10% fall. LVEF must be ≥50% before starting cycle 1.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel (cycle 1: starting day 1; docetaxel given day 2)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy (docetaxel cycles only)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose motion then 2mg after each loose motion (for diarrhoea - from support folder in Aria)
For infusion reactions (when required): chlorphenamine 10mg IV, hydrocortisone 100mg IV, paracetamol 1000mg oral
special_warnings: Indication: locally advanced or metastatic HER2 or FISH positive breast cancer. Prior adjuvant HER2 therapy must have been completed >12 months before diagnosis of metastatic disease. No prior chemotherapy or HER2 therapy in metastatic setting.
This protocol may require funding approval.
LVEF must be ≥50% before starting cycle 1 of pertuzumab and trastuzumab. Cardiac function assessed every 9 weeks and as clinically indicated.
Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pertuzumab or trastuzumab - if not tolerated, stop the drug.
Docetaxel: 6 cycles then pertuzumab and trastuzumab continued until disease progression or intolerance.
Diarrhoea can be severe with pertuzumab - ensure appropriate antidiarrhoeal therapy prescribed.
Pertuzumab associated with hypersensitivity/infusion reactions - observe 60 mins after first infusion, 30-60 mins after subsequent.
Trastuzumab associated with hypersensitivity reactions - observe 6 hours after first infusion, 2 hours after subsequent.
If pertuzumab gap ≥6 weeks, re-administer 840mg loading dose.
If trastuzumab missed by >14 days, re-administer 8mg/kg loading dose over 90 minutes.
Docetaxel: HER2-positive indication when anthracycline-containing therapy has failed or is contra-indicated.
Cardiotoxicity monitoring mandatory throughout treatment with pertuzumab and trastuzumab.

drugs.notes: LOADING DOSE (Cycle 1 only) - Pertuzumab 1200mg/Trastuzumab 600mg fixed combination (Phesgo). Subcutaneous injection over 8 minutes. Single syringe to single administration site. Alternate between left and right thigh only. Observe 30 minutes after loading dose. Administer before any taxane; observation period must be completed before starting docetaxel. If gap ≥6 weeks between injections, re-administer 1200mg/600mg loading dose.

MAINTENANCE DOSE (Cycles 2 onwards) - Pertuzumab 600mg/Trastuzumab 600mg fixed combination (Phesgo). Subcutaneous injection over 5 minutes. Single syringe to single administration site. Alternate between left and right thigh only. Observe 15 minutes after each maintenance dose. Administer before any taxane. Dose reductions NOT permitted - if not tolerated stop treatment. If gap <6 weeks, give maintenance dose as soon as possible on usual 3-weekly schedule.

Given cycles 1-6 only. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Doses >200mg in 500ml NaCl 0.9% (max concentration 0.74mg/ml). May be increased to 100mg/m² from cycle 2 if well tolerated. Exfoliant. Administer after pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC observation period is complete.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia grade 3-4 (previous dose 75mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 then reduce to 60mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia (previous dose 60mg/m²)

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until platelets ≥100x10^9/L then reduce to 60mg/m²
Dose reductions are NOT recommended. Patients may continue during reversible myelosuppression but must be monitored carefully for neutropenia complications. If treatment not tolerated, stop.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: AST/ALT ≥1.5xULN AND Alk Phos ≥2.5xULN
Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >ULN and/or AST/ALT ≥3.5xULN AND Alk Phos ≥6xULN

dose_modifications.notes: No specific dose adjustments recommended in hepatic impairment
No dose adjustment necessary. No recommendations available for severe renal impairment due to lack of data.

dose_modifications.condition: Left ventricular dysfunction: LVEF <40% or LVEF 40-45% with ≥10% fall from pre-treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold for at least 3 weeks. May resume if LVEF recovers to >45% or to 40-45% with <10% fall below pre-treatment. Discontinue if symptomatic heart failure confirmed - commence ACE inhibitor and refer to cardiologist.
Reduce from 75mg/m² to 60mg/m² once neuropathy resolves to grade ≤2. Consider stopping if grade 3 at <75mg/m² or grade 4.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel (docetaxel cycles 1-6 only)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy (docetaxel cycles only)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required (take home)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose motion then 2mg after each loose motion (for diarrhoea - from support folder in Aria)
For injection reactions (when required): chlorphenamine 10mg IV, hydrocortisone 100mg IV, paracetamol 1000mg oral
If dexamethasone pre-medication forgotten: dexamethasone 20mg IV once only 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy
special_warnings: Indication: HER2-positive metastatic or locally recurrent unresectable breast cancer; no previous anti-HER2 therapy or chemotherapy for metastatic disease.
LVEF must be ≥50% before starting treatment. LVEF assessed at baseline, prior to cycle 4, then every 4 cycles during maintenance monotherapy or as clinically indicated.
Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC - if not tolerated, stop treatment.
6 cycles of docetaxel with pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC, then pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC continued until disease progression or intolerance.
Pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC (Phesgo) is a fixed-dose combination: loading 1200mg/600mg, maintenance 600mg/600mg.
Administer pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC before any taxane; complete observation period before starting docetaxel.
Injection site must be alternated between left and right thigh only. New injections at least 2.5cm from previous site.
Diarrhoea can be severe - particularly with taxane concurrent treatment and in patients ≥65 years.
Severe pulmonary events reported with trastuzumab - caution in patients with dyspnoea at rest or concomitant taxane treatment.
Pertuzumab/trastuzumab SC associated with hypersensitivity/injection-related reactions - observe 30 minutes after loading dose, 15 minutes after maintenance doses.
If gap ≥6 weeks between injections, re-administer 1200mg/600mg loading dose.
Cardiotoxicity monitoring mandatory throughout treatment.
Docetaxel: doses >200mg must be diluted in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%.

drugs.notes: E100C phase only (cycles 1-3). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Vesicant. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 900mg/m². Discontinue if cardiac failure develops. Given BEFORE docetaxel cycles.
E100C phase only (cycles 1-3). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Neutral - not a vesicant. Dose banded per national dose bands.

Docetaxel phase only (cycles 4-6). 21-day cycle for 3 cycles. Intravenous infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Doses >200mg in 500ml NaCl 0.9% (max concentration 0.74mg/ml). Exfoliant. In patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting at 75mg/m² with view to increase to 100mg/m². The complete course of E100C is ALWAYS administered first, followed by docetaxel.

dose_modifications.condition: EC phase: neutrophils <1x10^9/L or platelets <100x10^9/L on day 1 (first episode)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay 7 days until criteria met. On subsequent cycles if counts below level, delay and/or consider reducing epirubicin to 75% of original dose. Cyclophosphamide dose intensity may be maintained.

dose_modifications.condition: EC phase: febrile neutropenia or delay due to neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L or platelets <50x10^9/L for >1 week
EC phase: second episode of neutropenia/thrombocytopenia
dose_modifications.notes: Further reduce to 50% or stop treatment

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 then reduce to 75mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Docetaxel: neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia (previous dose 75mg/m²)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until grade 1 then reduce to 60mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Docetaxel: neutropenia grade 4 or febrile neutropenia (previous dose 60mg/m²)

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce from 100mg/m² to 75mg/m². If at doses <100mg/m² or grade 4 develops, consider stopping.

Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L or AST >4xULN or bilirubin >51 µmol/L

dose_modifications.notes: Dose reduction may not be necessary

Hepatic impairment: Bilirubin >ULN and/or AST/ALT ≥3.5xULN AND Alk Phos ≥6xULN

pre_medications: E100C cycles (1-3): Aprepitant 125mg oral 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
E100C cycles (1-3): Dexamethasone 4mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
E100C cycles (1-3): Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
E100C take home: Aprepitant 80mg oral once a day for 2 days (starting day 2)
E100C take home: Dexamethasone 2mg twice a day for 3 days oral (starting day 2)
E100C take home: Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral
E100C take home: Ondansetron 8mg twice a day for 3 days oral (starting evening of day 1)
E100C growth factor: filgrastim 30 million units SC daily days 5-9, OR lenograstim 33.6 million units SC daily days 5-9, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2
Docetaxel cycles (4-6): Dexamethasone 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days starting 24 hours before docetaxel
Docetaxel cycles (4-6): Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy
Docetaxel take home: Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral
Docetaxel growth factor: filgrastim 30 million units SC daily days 3-9, OR lenograstim 33.6 million units SC daily days 3-9, OR pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 2
If dexamethasone docetaxel pre-medication forgotten: dexamethasone 20mg IV once only
special_warnings: Maximum lifetime cumulative dose of epirubicin is 900mg/m².
The complete course of E100C (3 cycles) is ALWAYS administered first and is followed by docetaxel (3 cycles) - sequential protocol.
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting treatment. Baseline LVEF should be measured, particularly in patients with history of cardiac problems or in the elderly.
Discontinue epirubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Epirubicin is a vesicant - extravasation precautions required.
Always check with the relevant consultant before delaying or reducing the dose in the adjuvant/neo-adjuvant setting.
Docetaxel is highly myelosuppressive; in patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting at 75mg/m² with view to increase to 100mg/m² if well tolerated.
Hypersensitivity reactions with docetaxel tend to occur with first or second infusion.
Dexamethasone pre-medication required for docetaxel phase to prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity.
Docetaxel doses >200mg must be diluted in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%.
Excessive lacrimation related to cumulative docetaxel doses (after median 400mg/m²).
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
